title: Chickpea in WANA Project: Concepts and Approaches
abstract:
Increasing food production continues to he as high a priority in rnany
national and international agricultural research programs, as in the
1960s and '70s. 'l'he task, however, has become more complex becnuse
agricultural technologies are now expected to he rnorr profitable,
stistainablc, and environment-friendly in addition to being highly
productive. Achieving this objective in SAT and in the dry arras of
WANA is particularly challenging because these environments are
poorly endowed for crop production. Also, the rnajority of farmers of
these rcgions havc small holdings, a poor resource base, and pract,ic:e
subsistence farming.
